summarization.a: The figure displays infrared absorption spectra at 300°C, after a TMA (a) dose and after a HF (b) dose.

data_extraction.a:
| Wavenumbers (cm⁻¹) | Absorbance after TMA dose | Absorbance after HF dose |
|-------------------|------------|------------|
| 1300              | 0.00      | 0.00 |
| 1220              | positive peak till 0.55|negative peak till -0.55|
| 1100              | 0.00      |0.00      |
| 1000              | 0.00       | broad negative peak till -0.02|
| 980               | broad negative peak till -0.55    | 0.00 |
| 850               | 0.00       | broad negative peak till -0.03|
| 800               | 0.00       | 0.00|

a.question: What does a positive or negative peak tell in an infrared spectrum?
a.answer: A positive peak means that bonds with corresponding vibrations are added to the surface, whereas a negative peak indicates the removal of bonds from the surface.

a.question: Is the same amount of CH3 added and removed each cycle?
a.answer: Yes, the positive and negative CH3 absorption peak are equaly big.

a.question: Assuming that AlF3 is added to the surface upon HF exposure, can it then be said that the baseline correction of the IR spectrum is not performed correctly?
a.answer: If AlF3 is added, this would result in a positive peak above 0, this is not observed, indicating that the baseline should likely be a bit higher.

a.question: Are the IR spectroscopy techniques that have sub-cycle accuracy?
a.answer: Yes, FTIR is an option on powder samples, and RAIRS can be used on planar reflective substrates.
